One fun way to build this skill is by playing short chess puzzles where you try to guess your opponent’s best response before making your move, almost like telling a little story in your head. This will help train your mind to keep track of several possible paths without feeling too overwhelming.

manual_anchors.anchor: Attends workshops on pottery for kids

You could set up a mini pottery station where kids roll and shape clay into animals, cups, or little sculptures, then paint them with bright colors once dried a bit. Add a short guided story or theme, like ‘forest creatures’ or ‘tiny villages,’ to spark their imagination. This keeps them hands-on and gives them something fun to take home.

manual_anchors.anchor: Enjoys sipping warm cocoa marshmallows

You could curl up under a blanket with a big mug of warm cocoa topped with lots of melty marshmallows, maybe even adding a sprinkle of cinnamon. If you want something different, you could also try warm vanilla milk or a cozy caramel-flavored hot chocolate. Either way, it’s perfect for a chilly evening when you want to feel safe and snug.
